summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for showing recent items. Microsoft Learn describes showRecentlyUsedFiles as a File Explorer general setting, the 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowRecent under both machine and current-user Explorer branches, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowRecent with both Data: 0 and Data: 1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Show Recent Items In Home
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer Home can show or hide recently used files.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable this when they want less activity history shown in Explorer Home, or enable it when they want quick access to recent files.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it exposes more recent-file history in Explorer Home, while disabling it reduces that history surface.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Learn describes showRecentlyUsedFiles as a File Explorer general setting. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowRecent under both the machine Explorer branch and the current-user Explorer branch, corroborating that the value family still exists on current builds. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowRecent = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowRecent = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Do not show recent items
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not show recently used files in Home. The Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Show recent items
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer shows recently used files in Home. The Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: The Microsoft settings reference describes the feature, while the 25H2 dump and Procmon probe corroborate the direct registry value and live Explorer read path.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app does not currently expose ShowRecent.

recommended_profiles.label: Show recent items
intended_for: Users who want recent files visible in Explorer Home
avoid_for: Users who want less activity history shown

states.rationale: This matches the runtime-observed Explorer read for the enabled state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want quick access to recently used files.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Explorer Home to expose less recent-file history.
tradeoffs: Shows recent files in Explorer Home.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide recent items
intended_for: Users who want less recent-file history shown
avoid_for: Users who rely on recent-file shortcuts

states.rationale: This matches the runtime-observed Explorer read for the disabled state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer Home to hide recent files.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on recent-file shortcuts in Explorer Home.
tradeoffs: Reduces recent-file history exposure in Explorer Home.
Removes quick access to recently used files from that surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: settings-common File Explorer general settings
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn describes showRecentlyUsedFiles as a File Explorer general setting and says it indicates whether recently used files are shown.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry corroboration for ShowRecent
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowRecent under both the machine Explorer branch and the current-user Explorer branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowRecent runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowRecent = 0 and ShowRecent = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowRecent with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: The 25H2 dump corroborates the ShowRecent value family, Microsoft Learn describes the feature, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference, but the direct first-party registry contract is weaker than ADMX-grade settings.

open_questions: Should the app expose ShowRecent as an Explorer Home privacy preference?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowRecent ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowRecent ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=True RESTORED_VALUE=0
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. The VM user baseline was 0 and was restored after the probe.
